records.ety: [Dim. of OE. timbre, OF. timbre; probably fr. L. typmanum, Gr. <?/ a kettledrum, but influenced perhaps by Ar. tabl a drum; cf. Per. tambal a drum. See Tympanum, and cf. 2d Timbre, Tymbal.]

defs: A kind of drum, tabor, or tabret, in use from the highest antiquity.
quotes: Miriam . . . took a timbrel in her hand, and all the women went out after her with timbrels and with dances.
